one_line_thesis: GPU cloud infrastructure landlord with $60.7B contracted backlog and $5.1B FY2025 revenue, but $25B debt, ~67% Microsoft concentration, and 6.2% base IRR at $73.21 → watch.

deep_dive.executive_summary: At $73.21, the base case is 6.2% per year over seven years on normalized starting owner cash of $1.25 per share (not raw operating cash flow). CoreWeave is the largest independent GPU cloud renter we cover, with FY2025 revenue $5.1 billion, Q1 2026 revenue $2.1 billion, and $60.7 billion of contracted backlog. Microsoft is ~67% of revenue; OpenAI and Meta have multi-billion-dollar master agreements. The business generates operating cash flow ($3.1 billion FY2025) but loses $1.2 billion at net income because interest expense ($1.2 billion) and depreciation overwhelm thin operating margins (o...

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: Predictable cash-flow security
applicability_tests: Incremental capital can be tied to identifiable projects, cohorts, or contracted capacity.
Returns on new capital can be separated from returns on the installed base.
Contract duration, volume protection, pricing, and counterparty risk are observable.
Leverage and future owner-funded capital are explicitly deducted.
failure_modes: contract renewal or regulatory reset ignored

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Operating-only fallback. Add an explicit component_valuation schedule before drawing asset-level conclusions.

valuation_proof.adjustment: Risk and timing are reflected in the low/base/high range.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Owner cash, reinvestment economics, or competitive position remains below the low-case path for two reporting periods.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

timeline.label: Operating income moved -85.8% versus the prior period (324.0 to 46.0).
Revenue moved +167.9% versus the prior period (1,915.0 to 5,131.0).
NVIDIA private placement ~$2.0B (~23M shares at $87.20) deepened strategic GPU supply partnership
FY2025 revenue $5.1B (+168% YoY) with $60.7B remaining performance obligations and $1.2B net loss
Meta order form up to ~$14.2B (later ~$21B with options) and OpenAI order form up to ~$6.5B expanded hyperscaler backlog
Announced acquisition of Core Scientific to integrate colocation and GPU fleet
IPO: 37M Class A shares at $40.00; legacy preferred converted; became public GPU cloud pure-play
OpenAI master services agreement with commitment up to ~$11.9B through October 2030
FY2024 revenue $1.9B; top two customers ~77%; net loss $863M as build-out accelerated
FY2023 revenue $229M; early-scale GPU cloud with net loss $594M

industry.structure: Specialized AI GPU cloud is oligopolistic at the buyer level (OpenAI, Microsoft, Meta) but fragmented at the provider level versus hyperscaler-owned capacity (AWS, Azure, GCP). CoreWeave competes as a neutral renter of NVIDIA clusters with first-access hardware relationships.
industry.share_shift: CoreWeave is gaining share in third-party GPU rental as hyperscalers outsource overflow training capacity; revenue grew from $229M (2023) to $5.1B (2025). Risk: hyperscalers can insource or multi-source, capping pricing power.
industry.trend: Exploding AI training and inference demand drives multi-year contract backlog, but capital intensity, GPU depreciation, and rising interest expense compress equity margins until utilization scales.

CoreWeave is the largest independent GPU cloud renter we cover, with FY2025 revenue $5.1 billion, Q1 2026 revenue $2.1 billion, and $60.7 billion of contracted backlog. Microsof

risk_claims.claim: Operating income moved -85.8% versus the prior period (324.0 to 46.0).
*Primary risk: Customer concentration (Microsoft ~67%) combines with $25 billion of debt so that any contract renegotiation, insourcing, or refinancing shock impairs equity despite headline backlog.

*Lens failure mode (levered): Treating $60.7 billion of remaining performance obligations as per-share net asset value, or using $3.1 billion of operating cash flow as equity free cash flow without subtracting interest, growth capital spending, and customer non-performance risk.
